title: China and Vietnam Vow to 'Better Navigate' South China Sea Frictions
detail: China and Vietnam agreed to 'better navigate' frictions in the South China Sea, signaling a diplomatic de-escalation effort between the two overlapping claimants. The agreement reflects both nations' interest in managing bilateral tensions without direct confrontation, even as structural disputes over fishing rights, oil exploration, and island features remain unresolved.

media.caption: China and Vietnam agree on diplomatic framework to manage South China Sea tensions

title: China Formally Protests New Zealand Aerial Patrols Near Chinese Coastline
detail: China lodged a formal diplomatic complaint accusing New Zealand of 'harassment' through aerial surveillance patrols conducted close to China's coastline. The protest escalates maritime surveillance tensions in the broader Indo-Pacific, as Five Eyes partners have increasingly conducted freedom of navigation and intelligence-gathering flights near Chinese territorial waters. New Zealand has not publicly commented on the specific complaint.

media.caption: China files formal protest over New Zealand surveillance flights near its coastline
